Responsibilities: Owns enterprise data governance policy; approves CDE designations; signs off on governance framework; escalation point for unresolved data quality issues.
Decision_Authority: Full authority over data policy, CDE definitions, and governance standards.

Responsibilities: Accountable for data within their business domain. Approves access requests, defines business rules, signs off on DQ thresholds, and accepts risk for their data assets.
Data_Assets_Owned: Domain-specific datasets (e.g., Housing Starts, Rental Market)
Decision_Authority: Approves/rejects access; sets acceptable DQ thresholds; accepts residual data risk.

Responsibilities: Day-to-day accountability for data quality. Reviews and resolves DQ exceptions. Documents business rules. Maintains data dictionary and CDE definitions. Liaison between IT and business.
Data_Assets_Owned: Specific datasets assigned by Data Owner
Decision_Authority: Resolves DQ issues within defined thresholds; escalates to Data Owner when issues exceed threshold.
Person_Title_Example: Senior Data Quality Analyst, Housing Market Analytics

Responsibilities: Responsible for the technical implementation of data governance controls. Manages data storage, access controls, ETL pipelines, and backup/recovery. Implements rules defined by Data Stewards.
Decision_Authority: Technical implementation decisions; must obtain Data Owner approval for schema changes.

Responsibilities: Uses data for analysis, reporting, or decision-making. Responsible for reporting data quality issues discovered during consumption. Must not modify source data.
Decision_Authority: None over data assets; may request access through formal process.
Person_Title_Example: Policy Analyst, Housing Research; BI Analyst

Responsibilities: Operates the governance program. Maintains governance framework documentation, Collibra catalog, training materials. Coordinates Data Council meetings. Monitors governance KPIs.
Data_Assets_Owned: Governance framework, policies, and catalog metadata
Decision_Authority: Governance process and policy; no authority over individual data content.
